originalAlarm.description: Undefined or garbage value returned to caller

sanitizedDescription: Undefined or garbage value returned to caller
presenceCondition:
(let ((a!1 (or (and (and (not __int8_t_defined) (not CONFIG_64BIT))
                    CONFIG_KTIME_SCALAR)
               (and (not __int8_t_defined) CONFIG_64BIT))))
(let ((a!2 (and (or (and (not CONFIG_64BIT) CONFIG_KTIME_SCALAR) CONFIG_64BIT)
                a!1)))
  (or a!2
      (and (not CONFIG_64BIT)
           (not CONFIG_KTIME_SCALAR)
           (and (not __int8_t_defined) (not CONFIG_64BIT))
           (not CONFIG_KTIME_SCALAR)))))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_64BIT () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_KTIME_SCALAR () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __int8_t_defined () Bool
  false)
reasoning: expires is assigned in both branches of the if-else at L55-58 (if: expires=ktime_add_ns(time_delta), else: expires=KTIME_MAX) before return expires at L60. Both branches always assign expires, so the return value is never garbage. The alarm is a false positive caused by restrictive library macro !__int8_t_defined in all clauses of the PC — stdint.h not processed, so s64/u64 typedefs are broken in the desugared analysis, causing CSA to mistrack expires.
